text:
CT SCAN OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST
Post-Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplant Surveillance Study

PATIENT: Marcus T. Delgado
CLINICAL INDICATION: 48-year-old male with known history of de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse risk, status post 7+3 induction chemotherapy with midostaurin achieving complete morphologic remission with MRD negativity. Subsequently underwent myeloablative conditioning with fludarabine/busulfan (FluBu4) followed by matched unrelated donor (MUD, 10/10 HLA-matched) allogeneic peripheral blood stem cell transplantation. Now approximately day +90 post-transplant. Surveillance imaging to evaluate for extramedullary relapse, organomegaly, or interval development of transplant-related complications. History of mild chronic GVHD affecting skin and oral mucosa, currently managed with tacrolimus taper and topical therapies.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast via antecubital vein at a flow rate of 3 mL/second. Acquisitions were obtained in the portal venous phase for the abdomen and pelvis and in the standard thoracic phase for the chest. No oral contrast was administered. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformations were generated and reviewed in conjunction with axial images. Comparison is made to the prior CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis obtained approximately day +0 to pre-transplant, as well as the prior response assessment CT performed approximately day +30 post-induction.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear throughout both lung fields. No areas of parenchymal consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or nodular density are identified. There are no pulmonary nodules. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally, with no endobronchial lesions or airway narrowing. No pleural effusions are seen bilaterally. The pleural surfaces are smooth without nodularity or thickening.

Mediastinum: The mediastinum is midline in position. The cardiac silhouette is within normal limits for size with no pericardial effusion. The great vessels are patent and of normal caliber. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal masses are identified. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy is present; the prevascular, paratracheal, subcarinal, and periesophageal nodal stations all appear unremarkable, with no discrete lymph node measuring pathologically enlarged. There is no hilar lymphadenopathy bilaterally.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues: The visualized chest wall musculature is symmetric and unremarkable. There are no soft tissue masses. Gynecomastia is absent. The thyroid gland, to the extent imaged at the thoracic inlet, appears homogeneous without focal lesions.

Axillae: Bilateral axillary regions show no discrete lymph node enlargement. Small subcentimeter lymph nodes are present bilaterally but are within normal morphologic limits.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement throughout without focal hypodense or hyperdense lesions. The hepatic contour is smooth. The liver craniocaudal span measures approximately 13.9 cm, well within normal range and a marked improvement compared to the presenting study (previously measured at 17.2 cm at initial staging) and comparable to the prior response assessment imaging following induction. There is no intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The common bile duct is not dilated (measuring approximately 4 mm in caliber). The portal vein is patent with normal flow-directing enhancement. The hepatic veins are patent and of normal caliber. No perihepatic fluid or ascites. No subcapsular collection. In the context of the patient's mild chronic GVHD with borderline transaminase elevations, no imaging correlate for hepatic GVHD (such as periportal edema or ductal irregularity) is identified, though imaging sensitivity for early hepatic GVHD remains limited.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: The gallbladder is present, non-distended, without wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. No gallstones. No choledocholithiasis.

Spleen: The spleen measures approximately 9.1 cm in its longest axis, which is within normal limits and represents sustained improvement from the presenting splenomegaly (previously 14.1 cm at initial staging). The splenic parenchyma enhances homogeneously with no focal lesions. No splenic infarcts. No perisplenic fluid.

Pancreas: The pancreas is visualized throughout its head, body, and tail with normal size and homogeneous enhancement. The pancreatic duct is not dilated. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid collections.

Adrenal Glands: The bilateral adrenal glands are of normal morphology and size. No adrenal nodules or masses.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size, shape, and symmetry of enhancement. There is brisk and symmetric nephrographic phase enhancement bilaterally. No focal renal masses or cysts of significance. No hydronephrosis or hydroureter. No perinephric stranding. The renal cortical thickness appears preserved bilaterally. No nephrolithiasis is identified on this contrast-enhanced study. Note is made of mild relative cortical thinning compared to the pre-treatment baseline, which may reflect the mild tacrolimus-related nephrotoxicity documented clinically.

Abdominal Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is of normal caliber without aneurysmal dilatation. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery are patent. The inferior vena cava is of normal caliber and patent.

Lymph Nodes — Abdomen: No retroperitoneal, para-aortic, aortocaval, or mesenteric lymphadenopathy is identified. Scattered small mesenteric lymph nodes are present but none exceeds 1 cm in short-axis diameter, and the overall nodal burden appears within reactive/normal limits. No discrete lymph node masses.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The stomach is partially distended and unremarkable. Small bowel loops are of normal caliber and wall thickness without evidence of obstruction, wall thickening, or mucosal abnormality. The colon and rectum are without significant distension, wall thickening, or pericolonic inflammation. No pneumatosis intestinalis. No free intraperitoneal air. No abdominal free fluid.

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is partially distended and demonstrates a smooth wall without focal thickening or filling defects.

Pelvic Soft Tissues and Organs: The prostate is normal in size and morphology. No pelvic free fluid. No pelvic soft tissue masses.

Lymph Nodes — Pelvis: No external iliac, obturator, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. No discrete pelvic lymph node enlargement.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:

The visualized osseous structures throughout the spine, ribs, sternum, clavicles, scapulae, pelvis, and proximal femora demonstrate no focal lytic or blastic lesions. No cortical destruction. No pathologic fracture. The bone density appears grossly preserved on this non-dedicated study. Of note, the medullary cavity demonstrates homogeneous, normal-appearing attenuation throughout the axial and appendicular skeleton, with no areas of focal marrow replacement, irregular marrow density, or cortical breakthrough that would suggest extraosseous extension of disease. This represents a favorable change from the presenting study, which demonstrated diffuse abnormal marrow signal compatible with leukemic infiltration.

SOFT TISSUES:

No subcutaneous or soft tissue masses. The abdominal and pelvic musculature is symmetric and unremarkable. No lipomatous or other soft tissue lesions of concern.

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of AML relapse or extramedullary leukemic disease. No chloroma or granulocytic sarcoma identified in any anatomic compartment.

2. Liver and spleen are both normal in size with homogeneous enhancement, representing complete resolution of the hepatosplenomegaly that was present at initial diagnosis and sustained resolution compared to the post-induction response imaging. No imaging features to suggest active hepatic graft-versus-host disease, though imaging has limited sensitivity for this entity.

3. No lymphadenopathy in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.

4. Lungs clear without consolidation, nodule, or pleural effusion — no evidence of pulmonary infection or pulmonary GVHD.

5. No new osseous lesions; the medullary spaces appear normal without focal marrow abnormality.

6. Overall unremarkable post-allogeneic transplant CT appearance at day +90, without imaging evidence of disease recurrence or significant transplant-related organ complication.
